evals.prompt: I want to make a video about extreme hot sauces, starting with mild ones and ending with the world's hottest. What format should I use and how should I structure it to maximize viewer retention all the way to the end?
evals.expected_output: Recommendation of the stair-stepping format with clear explanation of why it fits (progressive escalation, payoff at end, viewer curiosity about the next level). Should include: how to open, how to transition between sauces, what the stakes/payoff at the end look like, and a note on why the world-record sauce must be last.
expectations: Recommends the stair-stepping format by name or description
Explains that each heat level must be higher/more extreme than the last
States that the most extreme sauce must be revealed at the end (never early)
Includes ideas for what makes individual sauce segments entertaining beyond just eating
Notes a strong payoff structure at the finale (world's hottest sauce)

evals.prompt: We've done 8 'last to leave' challenge videos in the last year and viewership is declining on them. What should we do about the format? Give me specific advice on both short-term fixes and long-term strategy.
evals.expected_output: Advice that: diagnoses format fatigue, recommends separating same-format videos by at least 2-3 other formats, suggests innovating within the format (combine with stair-stepping, add twist), and potentially retiring it temporarily. Should reference the principle that viewers want new experiences even when they say they want more of the same.
expectations: Identifies this as format fatigue / format saturation
Recommends not running same format back-to-back (minimum 2-3 other formats in between)
Suggests either resting the format temporarily or adding a significant twist
References the principle that viewers don't actually want the same format forever
Provides at least one concrete innovation idea for making the format feel fresh

evals.prompt: Design a video format for this concept: 'A famous chef and I swap kitchens for a week — I cook in a Michelin-star restaurant, they cook in my home kitchen.' What format would make viewers watch to the end, what the payoff should be, and how to structure the story.
evals.expected_output: A format design that creates an open-loop question (who performs better? who succeeds or fails?), uses the outcome-unknown principle, structures the story as two parallel tracks that converge at the end, and designs a strong payoff (a taste test / judge / reaction moment). Should avoid just listing 'Day 1, Day 2...' structure.
expectations: Identifies an open-loop question that drives viewers to the end (who wins? who fails worse?)
Structures the video as parallel tracks that converge at payoff
Designs a specific payoff moment (e.g., blind taste test, professional judge, customer reactions)
Recommends showing early failures from both kitchens to build investment
Notes that the outcome must not be revealed before the payoff moment
